content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length N+1N+1N+1, denoted [A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN+1][A_1, A_2,\ldots, A_{N+1}][A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN+1‚Äč].

You can perform the following operation on the array AAA:


Choose an index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N) such that Ai‚Č§2‚čÖAN+1A_i \leq 2\cdot A_{N+1}Ai‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖAN+1‚Äč.

Then, swap the values of AiA_iAi‚Äč and AN+1A_{N+1}AN+1‚Äč.
That is, you can swap the element at index N+1N+1N+1 with some element of the array that's at most twice as large as it.

You may perform this operation as many times as you like (even zero times).

Find the **minimum** possible sum of **the first NNN elements** of AAA after the operations, i.e, the minimum possible value of

A1+A2+‚Ä¶+ANA_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_NA1‚Äč+A2‚Äč+‚Ä¶+AN‚Äč
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ meaning the length of the array is N+1N+1N+1.
The second line contains N+1N+1N+1 space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN,AN+1A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N, A_{N+1}A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč,AN+1‚Äč, the initial values of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible sum of AAA after performing some replacement operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, A=[5,2,1,2,3]A = [5, 2, 1, 2, 3]A=[5,2,1,2,3].
Perform one operation with i=1i = 1i=1, which is allowed since 5=A1‚Č§2‚čÖAN+1=65 = A_1 \leq 2\cdot A_{N+1} = 65=A1‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖAN+1‚Äč=6.
After the swap, A=[3,2,1,2,5]A = [3, 2, 1, 2, 5]A=[3,2,1,2,5].
The sum of the first NNN elements is 888, which is the minimum possible.
Test case 222: Initially, A=[3,10,30,1]A = [3, 10, 30, 1]A=[3,10,30,1]. AN+1A_{N+1}AN+1‚Äč cannot be swapped with anything since it's too small. So, the answer is just the sum of the first NNN elements, which is 3+10+30=433+10+30 = 433+10+30=43.
Test case 333: Initially, A=[16,4,8,2,1]A = [16, 4, 8, 2, 1]A=[16,4,8,2,1]. Consider the following sequence of operations:

Choose i=4i = 4i=4, which is valid since 2‚Č§2‚čÖ12 \leq 2\cdot 12‚Č§2‚čÖ1. Now, A=[16,4,8,1,2]A = [16, 4, 8, 1, 2]A=[16,4,8,1,2].
Choose i=2i = 2i=2, which is valid since 4‚Č§2‚čÖ24 \leq 2\cdot 24‚Č§2‚čÖ2. Now, A=[16,2,8,1,4]A = [16, 2, 8, 1, 4]A=[16,2,8,1,4].
Choose i=3i = 3i=3, which is valid since 8‚Č§2‚čÖ48 \leq 2\cdot 48‚Č§2‚čÖ4. Now, A=[16,2,4,1,8]A = [16, 2, 4, 1, 8]A=[16,2,4,1,8].
Choose i=1i = 1i=1, which is valid since 16‚Č§2‚čÖ816 \leq 2\cdot 816‚Č§2‚čÖ8. Now, A=[8,2,4,1,16]A = [8, 2, 4, 1, 16]A=[8,2,4,1,16].

The sum of the first NNN elements is 151515 which is the best we can do.
